message:
fix(io_uring): remove spurious early return when registrations are empty

Remove the guard that short-circuited submit_and_wait when there were no
active registrations. The intent was to avoid blocking forever on an empty
ring, but the logic was wrong in two ways:

1. It called ring.submit() (non-blocking) and returned Ok(0) regardless of
   whether a timeout was provided. When the caller passes Some(timeout)
   expecting the reactor to sleep until a timer fires, the early return
   caused a busy-spin: the caller saw 0 completions, re-entered tick(),
   hit the same guard, and looped at full CPU until a registration appeared.

2. The emptiness check on self.registrations was racy — a concurrent task
   could insert a registration between the lock check and the submit call,
   causing that submission's CQE to be missed on this tick.

The remaining match on timeout already handles the blocking/non-blocking
distinction correctly: None waits for at least 1 CQE, Some converts the
duration to a timespec and uses submit_and_wait_with_timeout. Both paths
naturally handle the empty-ring case because io_uring returns when the
timeout expires regardless of registration count.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
